one_line_takeaway: CFB borrows watermarking-style additive logit shaping to bias decoding toward input-context tokens, improving faithfulness in summarization and complementary-context QA without retraining, but it under-performs contrastive decoding under direct knowledge conflict and leans heavily on its semantic-similarity term.
key_finding: CFB is most effective when the task benefits from amplifying source-supported content. On NQ-Synth (complementary context), Token-aware CFB gives the best accuracy for all three models (60.10 Mistral-7B, 64.00 Llama2-13B, 73.40 Llama3-8B) and best ROUGE-L. On summarization it consistently beats baselines on CNN/DM and yields large FactKB gains on XSum (e.g., Llama3-8B FactKB rises to 66.85 with Context-aware CFB vs 45.77 for CAD). However, on NQ-Swap (direct knowledge conflict) the contrastive baseline ADACAD wins on accuracy for all three models (73.79/74.21/86.50), and CFB lags there, showing that simple additive boosting does not suppress parametric priors as strongly as contrastive methods when context and internal knowledge conflict. Human and GPT-4o judgments corroborate the automatic gains (Token-aware CFB: faithfulness 4.31, informativeness 4.12, fluency 4.18; GPT-4o consistency 0.91, hallucinations 0.67, contradiction 0.05, all best vs CAD/ADACAD).
core_contribution: The paper proposes Context-Fidelity Boosting (CFB), a decoding-time framework that reduces faithfulness hallucination (LLM outputs that contradict/ignore the provided input context) by adding a positive bias to the logits of tokens that appear in the input source span, thereby increasing the probability of generating context-supported tokens without any retraining or architectural change. The paper's own contribution is a three-level boosting mechanism of increasing adaptivity: (1) Static boosting adds a fixed bias delta to all source-supported tokens; (2) Context-aware boosting scales the bias by the Jensen-Shannon divergence between the next-token distributions computed with and without context, delta(D)=delta_min+(delta_max-delta_min)*D; and (3) Token-aware boosting keeps that sample-level adaptive boost but redistributes it across source tokens using a per-token relevance score r(w)=lambda1*attention(w)+lambda2*semantic_similarity(w) (lambda1=0.6, lambda2=0.4), then normalizes. The watermarking connection is only an inspiration/analogy for the logit-shaping idea (borrowed from Kirchenbauer et al. and related work, discussed in Related Work), NOT a watermark-detection or fingerprinting contribution of this paper.
threat_model: Not applicable. This is not a security paper. It addresses faithfulness hallucination (a reliability/quality problem in context-grounded generation such as RAG, summarization, and QA), not an adversarial threat. There is no attacker, no watermark to detect, and no security guarantee claimed. The word 'watermark' refers only to the logit-shaping technique the method is inspired by. No threat model is invented here.
limitations: Author-stated: (1) requires white-box access to logits, and for the token-aware variant to attention/embeddings, so it cannot be used with black-box APIs; (2) still adds decoding overhead from divergence computation and token-level relevance scoring (Token-aware CFB runtime is 10.39s/sample on CNN-DM vs 1.38s for Static CFB); (3) the token-aware variant depends on the quality of local relevance estimation and is not consistently superior in high-conflict settings. Reviewer-observed: the more elaborate Token-aware variant is not reliably the best (e.g., loses to ADACAD on all NQ-Swap accuracy and can hurt FactKB on XSum), and the ablation shows the method is fragile without its semantic-similarity component (ROUGE-L collapses from 35.81 to 4.45 when semantic similarity is removed), indicating the attention and JSD signals contribute comparatively little. Gains are largest exactly where the task is easy (context complements the model) and smallest where robust context-following matters most (conflict).

quantitative_results: CNN/DM Mistral-7B: Token-aware CFB best ROUGE-L 34.52 and FactKB 96.87 (CAD BERT-P 89.65 still best)
XSum Llama3-8B FactKB: Context-aware CFB 66.85 vs CAD 45.77
Human eval Token-aware CFB: faithfulness 4.31, fluency 4.18, informativeness 4.12 (1-5 scale, best on faith/info)
GPT-4o eval Token-aware CFB: consistency 0.91, hallucinations 0.67, contradiction rate 0.05 (all best)

Ablation (Llama3-8B, CNN-DM): full Token-aware CFB ROUGE-L 35.81 / FactKB 94.31 / BERT-P 89.38; w/o semantic collapses to ROUGE-L 4.45 / FactKB 66.84 / BERT-P 67.68; w/o attention 35.60; w/o JSD 35.24
Runtime per sample on CNN-DM: Static CFB 1.38s, Context-aware CFB 2.00s, Token-aware CFB 10.39s, COIECD 3.22s
Reasoning appendix (Llama3-8B): TriviaQA acc Token-aware CFB 71.5 vs CAD 40.2; HotpotQA CAD 39.5 remains best (CFB variants 34.7-37.1)
Hyperparameters: lambda1=0.6, lambda2=0.4; JSD bounded in [0,1] under base-2 log
baselines_compared: ADACAD (Adaptive Context-Aware Decoding, Wang et al. 2024)
COIECD (Contextual Information-Entropy Constraint Decoding, Yuan et al. 2024)
